Title: Defects in autophagy lead to selective in vivo changes in turnover of cytosolic and organelle proteins in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Autophagy core machinery component; ATG12-ATG5 conjugate/E3-like complex component
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ATG5 impairs autophagic flux and alters turnover/abundance of many cytosolic, ER, chloroplast and other proteins, with associated changes in amino acids and secondary metabolites under control and low-Pi conditions.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. atg5 mutants showed broad proteome remodeling and many proteins with slower degradation plus increased abundance, identifying selective autophagy cargo. 2. atg5 affected degradation of glycolytic enzymes including FBA8 and altered root/shoot metabolite profiles, notably lower amino acids and altered secondary metabolites. 3. Under Pi limitation, atg5 showed defective autophagy-associated responses including altered chloroplast protein turnover and metabolite changes.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Amino acids; secondary metabolites; salicylic acid conjugates; glucosinolates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutrient remobilization and stress-associated specialized metabolism
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mutant background analyzed in shoots and roots under control and phosphate limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; quantitative proteomics; 15N protein turnover analysis; metabolomics; confocal autophagy assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics and protein turnover MS: PXD010992; PXD010948; PXD010900; PXD010932
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Autophagy capacity is important for maintaining nutrient recycling and metabolic homeostasis; disrupting ATG5 is detrimental rather than beneficial for nutritional engineering.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Autophagy deficiency causes rapid senescence under nutrient limitation and broad metabolic disruption.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified here as Common Gene because it functions in autophagy machinery rather than as a pathway enzyme or transcriptional regulator.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ATG11 impairs selective autophagic degradation, especially of mitochondrial and chloroplast proteins, and alters amino acid and secondary metabolite profiles under control and low-Pi conditions.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. atg11 mutants showed slower degradation and increased abundance of many proteins, identifying ATG11-associated autophagy cargo. 2. ATG11 had a specialized role in mitochondrial and chloroplast protein homeostasis compared with ATG5. 3. Under Pi limitation, atg11 retained altered turnover of specific organellar proteins and exhibited distinct metabolite changes including altered asparagine and glucosinolate-related responses.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Amino acids; secondary metabolites; glucosinolates; salicylic acid conjugates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutrient remobilization and stress-associated specialized metabolism
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mutant background analyzed in shoots and roots under control and phosphate limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; quantitative proteomics; 15N protein turnover analysis; metabolomics; confocal autophagy assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics and protein turnover MS: PXD010992; PXD010948; PXD010900; PXD010932
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports the importance of selective autophagy for metabolic homeostasis; ATG11 is a mechanistic target but its disruption would likely be deleterious for crop nutrient recycling.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Autophagy deficiency causes rapid senescence under nutrient limitation and defective mitochondrial/chloroplast proteostasis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ATG11 showed a more specialized effect than ATG5 on mitochondrial and chloroplast protein degradation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for autophagic delivery of FBA8 to autophagic bodies in protoplast assays, supporting the assignment of FBA8 as autophagy cargo.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In atg7 protoplasts, FBA8-DsRed was not internalized into autophagosomes/autophagic bodies. 2. This provided functional evidence that FBA8 trafficking depends on core autophagy machinery. 3. ATG7 was also transcriptionally induced by Pi limitation in wild type and atg5 but not atg11.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced under phosphate limitation in wild type and atg5 but not atg11
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core autophagy gene useful for manipulating autophagic flux, but loss is likely detrimental to nutrient remobilization.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ProUBQ10 (constitutive) for GFP-ATG8a reporter line used in assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as supporting genetic evidence rather than the main experimental subject.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly cleaves fructose-1,6-bisphosphate into glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate and dihydroxyacetone phosphate in glycolysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Autophagy deficiency slows FBA8 degradation and causes its accumulation; FBA8 is a validated autophagy cargo.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FBA8 abundance increased and degradation rate decreased in atg5 and atg11 mutants. 2. FBA8-DsRed was delivered into GFP-ATG8a-positive autophagic bodies and vacuoles in wild-type protoplasts after AZD8055 treatment, but not in atg7. 3. FBA8 degradation was inhibited by E64d and concanamycin A, confirming autophagy-dependent vacuolar degradation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ProUBQ10 (constitutive) for GFP-ATG8a marker; pHBT promoter system for transient FBA8-DsRed expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Arabidopsis shoots/roots proteome; assessed in leaf protoplast assays
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; vacuolar autophagic bodies during autophagy
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate limitation; AZD8055; E64d; concanamycin A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein turnover measurement; protein abundance profiling; transient expression in protoplasts; colocalization with GFP-ATG8a; inhibitor assay; immunoblot
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary carbon metabolism supports synthesis of amino acids and specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics and protein turnover MS: PXD010992; PXD010948; PXD010900; PXD010932
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily a mechanistic marker for selective autophagy of glycolytic enzymes rather than a direct nutrient-improvement target.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen by authors as the representative newly identified glycolytic autophagy cargo among nine glycolytic enzymes.
